message:
fix(reaper): wrap all reaper mutations in explicit DOLT_COMMIT (gt-at0i)

The reaper previously relied on Dolt auto-commit, generating N*5 commits
per purge cycle (4 aux tables + 1 wisps per batch). This bloats the commit
graph that the Compactor must later flatten.

Now each reaper operation (reap, purge wisps, purge mail, auto-close issues)
disables auto-commit and issues a single DOLT_COMMIT per database. This
reduces commit graph growth from O(batches * tables) to O(databases).

Changes:
- reapWispsInDB: single commit for stale wisp closures
- purgeClosedWispsInDB: single commit for all batch deletes
- purgeOldMailInDB: single commit for all mail deletes
- autoCloseStaleIssuesInDB: single commit for all auto-closes
- autoCloseStaleIssuesSimple: single commit (fallback path)
- dolt-storage.md: update lifecycle status (all 6 stages implemented)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -184,7 +184,8 @@ CREATE → LIVE → CLOSE → DECAY → COMPACT → FLATTEN
 | COMPACT | Compactor Dog | Daily | `CALL DOLT_REBASE()` — squash old commits together |
 | FLATTEN | Mayor or manual | Monthly | Branch, soft-reset to initial commit, commit, swap main |
 
-The first three stages exist today. The last three are new work.
+All six stages are implemented. DECAY runs in the Reaper Dog (wisp_reaper.go),
+COMPACT/FLATTEN run in the Compactor Dog (compactor_dog.go).
 
 ### Two Data Streams
